name: Alanna McMurtrey
bio:
Alanna McMurtrey serves as the Lead Case Manager for the Southern California facilities of Quadrant Health Group, where she oversees case management services and supports clients through detox and residential levels of care. In her role, she coordinates client care, provides leadership and clinical support to case management staff, and helps ensure that each individual receives consistent, structured, and personalized support throughout their treatment journey.
With several years of experience in behavioral health and addiction treatment, Alanna has developed a strong passion for helping individuals overcome substance use disorders and co-occurring mental health challenges. She is dedicated to fostering engagement in treatment, promoting personal growth, and supporting clients as they work toward sustainable, long-term recovery.
Alanna takes a client-centered, strengths-based approach to care, meeting individuals where they are and helping them build upon their unique strengths. She is committed to creating a safe, respectful, and supportive environment where clients feel heard, valued, and empowered to make meaningful changes in their lives. Through collaboration, compassion, and clinical consistency, she strives to help clients develop the skills, confidence, and stability needed to achieve lasting recovery and improved well-being.

name: Alicia Joslin
bio:
With a deep passion for recovery and mental health care, Alicia Joslin brings empathy, leadership, and dedication to her role as Program Director at Marina Harbor Detox. She works closely with both clients and staff to foster an environment centered on accountability, compassion, and personal growth.
Alicia understands that recovery is not one-size-fits-all and believes every client deserves individualized support and encouragement throughout their treatment journey. Her goal is to help clients feel supported during some of the most difficult moments of their lives while empowering them to move toward lasting recovery and stability.

name: Angela Taylor
role: Case Manager at Hillside Mission Recovery
bio:
Angela Taylor, serves as a Case Manager dedicated to supporting individuals throughout their recovery journey. With experience in the substance use treatment field, Angela is passionate about helping clients navigate the treatment process with greater confidence, comfort, and purpose while fostering personal growth and long-term success.
Known for her compassionate and supportive approach, Angela strives to make the recovery experience as meaningful and stress-free as possible. She is committed to helping clients access the resources, guidance, and encouragement they need to build a strong foundation for lasting change.
Angela is currently pursuing a degree in Human Services and plans to continue advancing her education to further enhance her ability to serve individuals, families, and communities impacted by substance use and behavioral health challenges.

name: Anom Grant
bio:
As an IT Director with over a decade of experience, I bring a distinctive approach to technology leadership. My background in Psychology isn't just a detail; it's the very foundation of my career transition into IT. I firmly believe that technology should empower, not complicate, the human experience at work. This unique perspective drives me to transform tech landscapes, focusing on efficiency and innovation by ensuring IT initiatives truly align with business goals and build robust, scalable systems that enhance both organizational growth and overall user well-being.
My core strengths lie in dynamic team leadership, strategic project management, and comprehensive system optimizations. I consistently apply these skills to refine our technological infrastructure and streamline workflows. This ensures our IT operations are not only resilient and reliable but also directly contribute to achieving the organization's strategic objectives, making technology a true enabler. Moving forward, I'm passionate about leveraging my expertise to deepen organizational efficiency in ways that genuinely uplift both our internal teams and our valued clients. My aim is to implement tech solutions that optimize performance while simultaneously fostering a seamless, intuitive, and highly productive experience for everyone. I'm always eager to connect with fellow leaders to explore how thoughtful IT strategy can spark innovation and significantly boost user satisfaction.

name: Antoine Gross Sr.
role: Director of Clinical Services - Dallas Detox Center
bio:
Antoine is an independently licensed counselor with nearly a decade of experience in the mental health field. Over the past three years, he has provided independent counseling services, supporting individuals who are committed to creating meaningful and lasting change in their lives.
Antoine is deeply passionate about walking alongside individuals during some of their most challenging and transformative seasons. He believes therapy should be a safe, welcoming space where clients can openly explore their identity, experiences, and struggles without judgment—while also being thoughtfully challenged to grow.
His clinical approach is collaborative and practical, focusing on identifying patterns, building effective coping strategies, and developing the skills necessary for long-term success. Antoine's goal is for each client to leave treatment feeling empowered, balanced, and equipped to maintain continued growth well beyond their time in therapy.
He firmly believes in the principle: 'You can be great by chance, or you can be great by choice,' and is dedicated to helping individuals intentionally choose a path toward growth, healing, and fulfillment.

name: Ashley Hurtado
bio:
Ashley Hurtado, is an Associate Marriage and Family Therapist dedicated to helping individuals navigate the early stages of recovery with compassion, understanding, and clinical excellence. As a therapist at Marina Harbor Detox, Ashley works closely with clients as they begin their healing journey, providing trauma-informed care and individualized support during one of the most transformative periods of treatment.
Ashley earned her Master of Arts in Clinical Psychology from Pepperdine University and her Bachelor of Arts in Psychology from San Francisco State University. Her educational background, combined with her commitment to client-centered care, allows her to help individuals address the emotional, psychological, and behavioral challenges that often accompany substance use disorders and co-occurring mental health concerns.
In her role, Ashley facilitates individual and group therapy sessions, helping clients develop insight into the underlying factors contributing to their struggles while building healthier coping skills and emotional resilience. She is passionate about creating a safe, supportive therapeutic environment where clients feel heard, respected, and empowered to make meaningful changes in their lives.
Ashley's clinical approach is collaborative, strengths-based, and rooted in meeting each person where they are. She believes that lasting recovery begins with genuine connection and that every individual has the capacity for growth, healing, and transformation. Through empathy, encouragement, and evidence-based therapeutic practices, she helps clients build a strong foundation for sustained recovery and long-term well-being.
Ashley is committed to supporting clients through every stage of the recovery process, helping them cultivate hope, confidence, and the tools needed to create healthier, more fulfilling lives.

name: Christi Llamas
bio:
Christi Llamas is a dedicated Case Manager whose passion for helping others is rooted in both personal and professional experience. Having grown up in a family impacted by mental health and substance use challenges, and later navigating her own recovery journey, Christi brings a unique level of empathy, understanding, and authenticity to her work.
Her lived experience has given her firsthand insight into the complexities of addiction, recovery, and the barriers many individuals face when seeking support. Through overcoming challenges associated with substance use and involvement with the criminal justice system, Christi developed a deep commitment to helping others find hope, stability, and a path forward.
As a Case Manager, Christi is dedicated to building meaningful connections with clients, advocating for their needs, and helping them access the resources and support necessary for lasting recovery. She is passionate about empowering individuals to recognize their strengths and create positive change in their lives.
Christi is currently pursuing her education with the long-term goal of becoming a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW), allowing her to further expand her knowledge and ability to support individuals and families affected by substance use and mental health disorders.

name: Christina Lilly
bio:
Christina Lilly serves as the Office Manager at Wellness Recovery Center, where she plays a vital role in ensuring the day-to-day operations of the facility run smoothly and efficiently. Since joining the team in 2024, Christina has become a central point of support for all departments, helping to maintain organization, communication, and overall workflow across the organization.
In her role, Christina oversees administrative operations, coordinates office procedures, and provides ongoing support to clinical, case management, and leadership teams. She is known for her strong ability to multitask, prioritize responsibilities, and respond quickly to the evolving needs of both staff and clients.
A natural people-person, Christina brings empathy and approachability into every interaction. She takes pride in being accessible and responsive, fostering a supportive and collaborative environment throughout the workplace. Her problem-solving skills allow her to effectively address challenges as they arise, ensuring minimal disruption to daily operations.
Christina is deeply committed to helping Wellness Recovery Center function at its highest level, consistently going above and beyond to support staff, enhance efficiency, and contribute to a positive, client-centered atmosphere.

name: Cristina Turiello
bio:
Cristina Turiello is a kind, compassionate, and highly skilled behavioral health professional with over five years of experience at Quadrant Health Group. As the Director of Client Care, she leads a team dedicated to supporting individuals facing substance use and mental health challenges, ensuring they receive personalized, respectful, and effective care.
Cristina's journey in healthcare began as a patient advocate, where she championed the rights and well-being of individuals navigating complex systems. Her deep-rooted passion for helping others in all aspects of life continues to drive her work and leadership style today.
With a strong foundation in organization, attention to detail, and team leadership, Cristina brings structure and clarity to challenging environments while maintaining a warm, client-centered approach. She is known not only for her operational expertise but for her unwavering kindness, empathy, and ability to connect meaningfully with both clients and colleagues.
Cristina remains committed to making a difference—one compassionate interaction at a time.

name: Deborah Wade
bio: Deborah has been a dedicated member of the Dallas Detox Center team for the past three years. With a strong passion for client care and recovery, Deborah plays a vital role in medically supporting clients through the detoxification process. She also manages client care at the Residential Treatment Center (RTC) level of care and provides leadership and oversight to the nursing staff. Her compassionate approach and clinical expertise help ensure a safe, supportive environment for clients at every stage of their recovery journey.

name: Denise Edwards
bio:
Denise Edwards is a mental health professional with over 17 years of experience working with individuals facing substance use and co-occurring mental health challenges. Denise has served in roles spanning administration, admissions, case management, counseling, and advocacy, and spent two years supporting survivors of domestic violence.
Denise holds a Master of Social Work (MSW) and is currently pursuing licensure as an LSW on the path to becoming an LCSW. Denise's work is grounded in a commitment to reducing stigma and supporting recovery through compassionate, client-centered care.

name: Dr. Olivia M. Gibson-Delaney, M.D.
bio: Dr. Olivia Gibson-Delaney earned her medical degree from Rutgers New Jersey Medical School in Newark, NJ. She completed her Psychiatry residency at Mount Sinai Morningside and Mount Sinai West in New York, NY, where she developed expertise in diagnosing and treating a wide range of mental health conditions. Dr. Gibson-Delaney is dedicated to providing compassionate, evidence-based care to her patients and is committed to advancing mental health awareness and treatment in her community.

name: Elizabeth Wald
role: Director of Operations - Ocean Coast Recovery Center
bio:
I currently serve as the Director of Operations at Ocean Coast Recovery Center, where I am responsible for overseeing the daily operations of the facility. My role includes managing staff, developing and implementing policies and procedures, and ensuring full compliance with industry regulations and standards.
I've been working in the addiction treatment field since 2021. I've proudly been part of the team since the facility's opening. I've continued my work here since that time. What I appreciate most about being part of a small, six-bed facility is the family-like atmosphere it creates. It allows us to truly focus on each individual's needs and provide meaningful, personalized support.
Professionally, I'm passionate about creating a space where people feel heard, supported, and empowered. I entered this field because I understand firsthand what our clients are going through, I've walked that path myself. My own recovery journey gives me insight into the challenges they face. My Job is about meeting people where they are, equipping them with the tools, knowledge, and encouragement they need to pursue lasting sobriety

name: Emma Fyffe
bio: Emma is a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) and a compassionate, client-centered mental health professional. She serves as the primary therapist at Greater Texas Behavioral Clinic, an intensive outpatient program providing care for individuals experiencing mental health and substance use challenges. In her role, Emma facilitates both individual and group therapy, supporting clients as they build insight, develop healthier coping skills, and work toward sustainable recovery. She values creating a safe, nonjudgmental environment where clients feel heard, supported, and empowered. Her approach emphasizes emotional awareness, accountability, and practical tools to promote long-term growth and stability.

name: Erika "Riky" Hanaumi
credentials: Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW); Master of Social Work (MSW), California State University, Fullerton (2013); trained in evidence-based modalities including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT)
bio:
Erika “Riky” Hanaumi is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker with more than 20 years of experience in behavioral health and addiction treatment. She currently serves as Clinical Director for Quadrant Health Group’s California facilities, where she oversees clinical programming, mentors and supports therapists in developing effective treatment strategies, and ensures the delivery of compassionate, individualized, and clinically sound care. Her leadership focuses on promoting evidence-based practices, clinical excellence, and positive treatment outcomes for individuals with co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders.
Riky began her career working with individuals experiencing homelessness, providing intensive support, advocacy, and resource coordination to help clients overcome barriers and access essential services. This experience fueled her passion for serving vulnerable populations and inspired her to pursue a Master of Social Work degree from California State University, Fullerton, which she earned in 2013.
Throughout her career, Riky has worked in both inpatient and outpatient settings, providing therapy, crisis intervention, case management, and recovery-oriented services. She has extensive experience supporting adults with complex behavioral health needs, including co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders, while helping individuals navigate the challenges of recovery and major life transitions.
Riky is trained in evidence-based treatment modalities, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT). She is passionate about helping clients build resilience, strengthen coping skills, improve interpersonal relationships, and achieve lasting recovery.

name: Erika Sirianos
bio:
With over 20 years of progressive leadership experience, Erika Sirianos is a seasoned Human Resources and Payroll Director with a dynamic background spanning both the nonprofit and corporate sectors. Her expertise lies in aligning people strategies with organizational goals, ensuring compliance, and fostering inclusive workplace cultures that drive performance and engagement.
Throughout her career, Erika has led HR and payroll operations in diverse environments, including behavioral health and substance abuse treatment organizations as well as educational institutions. Her hands-on experience navigating complex regulatory frameworks, managing multi-state payroll systems, and implementing HR best practices uniquely positions her to support mission-driven and growth-oriented organizations alike.
Known for her collaborative approach and strong ethical compass, Erika has a proven track record in talent management, benefits administration, policy development, and organizational change. She is especially passionate about shaping people and culture strategies that cultivate belonging, accountability, and continuous improvement.

name: Erin Crawford
credentials: Registered Nurse (RN); Bachelor of Science in Nursing (Chamberlain University); currently pursuing Master of Science in Nursing with a Family Nurse Practitioner (FNP) concentration
bio:
Born in Okinawa, Japan and raised in South Florida, Erin Crawford is a dedicated and compassionate Registered Nurse with a strong commitment to serving individuals affected by substance abuse, mental health challenges, and health disparities within our communities. She is a proud mother of two and finds balance between her professional and personal life. When she's not cheering her children on at the soccer pitch, Erin enjoys relaxing at the beach with her beloved dog.
Erin graduated with President's Distinguished Honors from Chamberlain University, earning her Bachelor of Science in Nursing. She is currently pursuing her Master of Science in Nursing with a concentration as a Family Nurse Practitioner (FNP), continuing her mission to broaden her impact in patient care and advocacy.
With a deep-rooted passion for helping others, Erin focuses her nursing career on empowering individuals facing addiction and mental health challenges. She is a fierce advocate for equitable care and works to uplift those experiencing social, economic, and healthcare disparities. Erin brings empathy, strength, and dedication to every role she takes on—driven by a desire to be a source of support and hope for those most in need.

name: Gus Saadeh
bio:
As the Director of Operations at Marina Harbor Detox, Gus Saadeh manages and oversees the day-to-day operations with a strong focus on facilities maintenance, Health & Safety compliance, and staff coordination. Working closely with the Program Director, Gus ensures staff are trained effectively and that services are delivered with professionalism, compassion, and alignment with industry best practices.
Gus's path into this field is deeply personal—being in recovery himself provides a unique understanding of the challenges clients face in early sobriety. This lived experience drives his commitment to creating an environment that is safe, respectful, and grounded in genuine hope. Every operational decision Gus makes is guided by a client-centered mindset, knowing firsthand how critical stability and empathy are during the early stages of recovery.
Before stepping into this role, Gus worked as a licensed realtor in San Francisco. That experience taught him how to connect with people from all walks of life, build trust quickly, and communicate with empathy—skills that now serve him well in supporting clients, families, and staff in a treatment setting. He has also worked closely with aging individuals and their families on issues of trust, long-term planning, and transitions of care, bringing sensitivity and structure to complex and emotional situations.
Gus's areas of expertise include recovery-based operational leadership, staff and facilities management, and maintaining Health & Safety compliance. He is also skilled in providing empathetic support to clients and their families and has a strong background in transitional planning for aging adults and their loved ones. These combined strengths allow him to bring a balanced approach of structure, compassion, and practical guidance to every aspect of his role.

name: Haley Wadlington
bio:
Haley Wadlington serves as the Director of Client Care, where she is dedicated to ensuring that each client receives compassionate, individualized, and consistent support throughout their treatment experience. In her role, Haley helps oversee client care coordination, discharge planning, family communication, and ongoing support services to help clients feel seen, heard, and guided during a vulnerable time in their lives.
Haley is passionate about creating a client-centered environment built on dignity, accountability, and meaningful connection. She works closely with clients, families, and the clinical team to help identify needs, reduce barriers, and support a smooth transition into the next appropriate level of care. Her approach emphasizes advocacy, clear communication, and practical support, ensuring that clients are not only cared for during treatment but also prepared for continued success after discharge.
With a strong commitment to ethical care and trauma-informed support, Haley strives to make every client interaction purposeful, respectful, and rooted in hope. Her goal is to help clients and their loved ones feel supported, informed, and empowered as they move forward in their recovery journey.

name: Halie Nall
role: Case Manager at Ocean Coast Recovery Center
bio:
Halie Nall is a dedicated Case Manager who is passionate about supporting individuals throughout their recovery journey and helping them access the resources, tools, and support needed to build healthy, fulfilling lives. Her commitment to this work is shaped by both professional experience and personal lived experience with addiction and mental health challenges, as well as witnessing the impact of substance use within her own family.
These experiences have provided Halie with a deep sense of empathy, understanding, and compassion for those facing similar struggles. She believes that recovery is possible for everyone and is committed to meeting clients where they are, offering encouragement, advocacy, and support every step of the way.
Known for her compassionate approach and genuine connection with clients, Halie strives to create a safe and supportive environment where individuals feel heard, valued, and empowered as they work toward lasting recovery and personal growth.

name: Ila Holgerson
bio:
Ila is the Lead Case Manager at Wellness Recovery Center, where she has been a dedicated member of the team since 2023. Originally from Gilbert, Arizona, Ila brings a compassionate and client-centered approach to her work, focusing on helping individuals navigate the practical challenges that can impact their recovery journey.
In her leadership role, Ila oversees a team of case managers who provide support in areas such as legal coordination, housing, employment, and aftercare planning. She prides herself on her strong problem-solving skills, using them to develop effective, individualized solutions that empower clients to focus on healing and long-term stability. Ila is deeply committed to creating a supportive environment where clients feel heard, understood, and guided every step of the way.

name: Jaclyn Paradise
bio:
Jaclyn Paradise is a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) and Certified Trauma Professional who works with adults navigating trauma, anxiety, life transitions, and recovery. She earned her master's degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Rider University and has extensive experience in both private practice and intensive outpatient settings, supporting individuals with co-occurring mental health and substance use challenges.
Jaclyn's approach is holistic and trauma-informed, integrating parts work, mindfulness, and body-based practices to help clients reconnect with themselves and feel safe in their own bodies. She is especially passionate about inner child healing, self-empowerment, and guiding clients through meaningful life changes.
Drawing from both clinical training and personal insight, Jaclyn creates a grounded, supportive space where clients can explore, grow, and develop a deeper understanding of who they are.

name: Jacob Cameron
credentials: Registered Substance Use Disorder Counselor (SUDCC I)
bio:
Jacob Cameron serves as the Client Care Director at Quadrant Health Group and is a Registered Substance Use Disorder Counselor (SUDCC I). Passionate about helping individuals navigate the recovery process, Jacob is dedicated to creating a treatment experience that is both meaningful and engaging. He believes that lasting recovery is built through genuine connection, compassionate support, and an environment where clients feel valued every step of the way.
In his role, Jacob works to ensure that each client receives personalized care and experiences a sense of belonging throughout their treatment journey. His goal is to help individuals not only achieve recovery but also discover that life in recovery can be fulfilling, rewarding, and enjoyable. Through his commitment to client-centered care, Jacob strives to make a lasting positive impact on the lives of those he serves.

name: Jada Spencer
bio:
Jada brings over five years of experience in mental and behavioral health, driven by a genuine passion for supporting individuals through life's most challenging seasons. She is committed to building meaningful, authentic connections so clients feel safe, supported, and comfortable being open throughout their treatment journey. As a Case Manager, Jada helps clients navigate everyday stressors while identifying practical next steps toward stability and long-term growth.
Wellness plays a central role in her approach. Through her own grounding routines, including Pilates, Jada has developed a deep appreciation for the connection between physical and emotional regulation. She encourages clients to explore movement, structure, and self-care practices that promote balance and reduce stress. Her goal is for every individual she works with to leave treatment feeling more stable, confident, and prepared for the road ahead.

name: Jeremiah Ross
bio:
Jeremiah Ross, is a dedicated healthcare professional with more than 10 years of patient care experience and a strong background in substance use disorder treatment, client care coordination, and clinical team leadership. As the Nursing Supervisor, Jeremiah plays an integral role in supporting both clients and staff, helping oversee day-to-day clinical operations, medication-assisted treatment (MAT) protocols, documentation compliance, staff development, and multidisciplinary collaboration to ensure the highest standards of care.
Passionate about helping individuals navigate the recovery process, Jeremiah is committed to creating a safe, supportive, and structured treatment environment where clients can build stability, develop healthy coping skills, and work toward lasting recovery. His leadership style emphasizes compassion, accountability, and teamwork, helping foster positive outcomes for both clients and clinical staff.

name: Joshua Leder
bio:
Joshua Leder serves as Director of Operations for the Texas facilities under Quadrant Health Group, where he oversees daily operations and supports the continued growth and development of the organization's behavioral health programs across the state.
Originally born in Colorado and raised primarily in Texas, Joshua brings both personal passion and professional dedication to his role. As a proud father of two children, he values family, purpose, and creating a positive impact in the lives of others.
Joshua is deeply committed to helping individuals build healthier, more fulfilling lives while encouraging hope, healing, and personal growth. Guided by compassion, understanding, and faith, he strives to support others in recognizing that lasting change and recovery are possible. Drawing from his own experiences and journey, Joshua is passionate about inspiring those around him through strength, empathy, and service to others.

name: Justin White
bio:
Justin White is a Registered Addiction Counselor with a passion for helping individuals find hope, healing, and long-term stability in recovery. With experience working in both detox and residential treatment settings, Justin is dedicated to creating a safe, supportive environment where clients feel heard, respected, and empowered throughout their recovery journey.
Justin believes recovery is not one-size-fits-all and values building genuine connections with each client through compassion, honesty, and understanding. He is committed to helping individuals navigate the challenges of addiction and co-occurring mental health disorders while supporting the development of healthier habits, stronger coping skills, and renewed confidence.
Known for his approachable and supportive nature, Justin strives to make a meaningful impact on the lives of the clients and families he works with each day. His goal is to help individuals recognize their potential for growth and lasting change while reminding them they never have to face recovery alone.

name: Karen Pettit
bio:
As a passionate advocate for mental health and substance abuse treatment, I have dedicated my career to helping those in need. My journey began with personal experiences of loss and witnessing the struggles of loved ones who faced mental health challenges and substance abuse. This profound understanding ignited a desire within me to make a meaningful impact in the lives of others.
I hold an Associate's degree in Alcohol and Drug Studies from Saddleback College, which I completed in 2024, and I am proud to have passed the state exam for my Substance Use Disorder Certified Counselor (SUDCC II) credential. Currently, I am pursuing a bachelor's degree in Psychology to deepen my understanding and enhance my ability to support those on their journey to recovery.
“A little consideration, a little thought for others, makes all the difference.” – Eeyore.  I embody this belief in my work, committed to providing the care and guidance that individuals and families need, drawing from both my professional training and my personal experiences.

name: Lamont Damon, AMFT
bio:
Lamont Damon is an Associate Marriage and Family Therapist dedicated to helping individuals navigate substance use recovery and behavioral health challenges with compassion, understanding, and evidence-based care. With experience working in detoxification and residential treatment settings, Lamont facilitates process groups, mindfulness groups, and trauma-informed therapeutic interventions designed to support healing and personal growth.
His clinical approach is rooted in creating a safe, supportive environment where clients can openly explore their emotions, strengthen recovery skills, develop healthy coping strategies, and build greater self-awareness. Drawing from person-centered, cognitive behavioral, mindfulness-based, and trauma-informed modalities, Lamont tailors his work to meet each individual's unique needs and goals.
Passionate about empowering individuals on their recovery journey, Lamont strives to help clients build resilience, foster meaningful change, and establish a strong foundation for long-term wellness and sustained recovery.

name: Michael McArthur
bio:
I am the Director of Nursing for California facilities, overseeing all medical staff and client care operations. My journey into nursing was inspired by a personal desire to provide hope and compassion during life's most challenging moments. Watching nurses care for my family during a difficult time, I realized how powerful a little hope and dedicated care can be. This inspired me to pursue a career in nursing, knowing I could make a difference when people need it most.
I love working in addiction recovery because it allows me to witness clients grow and thrive within our walls. Our staff's client-focused approach creates a positive, motivating environment that makes coming to work truly rewarding.
Outside of my professional life, I am a proud father of four wonderful kids. They are my greatest inspiration—teaching me patience, resilience, and the importance of hope every day. My personal recovery journey has also strengthened my understanding of overcoming adversity, and it fuels my dedication to helping others find their path to healing.
I work in this industry because I believe that substance use disorder and behavioral health are underserved populations that deserve attention, compassion, and support. I am excited to be on the front lines helping to reduce stigma and provide clients with genuine opportunities for recovery.
Personally, I am passionate about my family, continuous improvement in patient care through scientific and technological advances, and my love for the Los Angeles Lakers. My goal is to make a meaningful difference, one life at a time, with hope, compassion, and unwavering dedication.

name: Michael Meagher
credentials: MSW, LCSW, CAP; Master of Social Work from Samford University; Licensed Clinical Social Worker and Certified Addiction Professional in the state of Florida; EMDR trained
bio:
Michael Meagher, MSW, LCSW, CAP, is the Clinical Director with more than 13 years of experience in the behavioral health and substance use treatment field. He holds a Master of Social Work from Samford University and is both a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and Certified Addiction Professional in the state of Florida. Michael is also EMDR trained and specializes in co-occurring disorders, trauma-informed care, and addiction treatment.
Throughout his career, Michael has worked across multiple levels of care, beginning in recovery support services before advancing into counseling, operations, and executive clinical leadership. As Clinical Director, he oversees clinical programming, staff supervision, regulatory compliance, utilization review, and multidisciplinary treatment coordination. He is passionate about creating individualized, evidence-based treatment experiences that support long-term recovery and meaningful personal growth. 'This is not just my profession, it's my passion.'

name: Michelle Szwed
credentials: LPC, LCADC, ACS, BCC, NCC, CCTP, BA
bio:
Michelle Szwed is a Licensed Professional Counselor and Licensed Clinical Alcohol and Drug Counselor with experience treating co-occurring disorders, addiction, trauma, and substance use. She has worked across private practice, partial hospitalization, and intensive outpatient settings, where she has developed a strong clinical foundation through hands-on experience, collaboration with multidisciplinary teams, and a commitment to ongoing professional growth.
Michelle is known for her empathetic and grounded approach, creating a therapeutic environment where clients feel supported, understood, and empowered. Her clinical strengths include active listening, thoughtful communication, strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to foster meaningful engagement in both individual and group settings. She is especially skilled at leading interactive psychoeducational and process groups while maintaining an authentic, down-to-earth presence.
In her work, Michelle draws from a range of evidence-based modalities, including DBT, Motivational Interviewing, Internal Family Systems, EMDR, Cognitive Processing Therapy, relapse prevention, and expressive arts therapies. She is trained in both EMDR and Brainspotting and specializes in supporting individuals navigating trauma, borderline personality disorder, and substance use challenges.
In addition to her counseling credentials, Michelle is a board-certified life coach (BCC), nationally certified counselor (NCC), certified clinical trauma professional (CCTP), and certified behavior assistant (BA), reflecting her dedication to providing comprehensive, compassionate care.

name: Monica Olivires
credentials: CADII (Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor II / CADC II)
bio:
Hi, I'm Monica Olivares — Program Director at Hillside Mission and a firm believer that healing doesn't have to be boring. With over 11 years in the behavioral health field, I bring clinical experience, leadership, humor, heart, and a whole lot of hustle to the work I love. I've worked in just about every setting imaginable — Detox, Residential, IOP, PHP, OP — and in nearly every role, from graveyard tech, lead tech, biofeedback therapy, to case manager, assistant manager, program manager, and now, program director.
I hold a CADC II certification and have a background of working with clients facing substance use, co-occurring disorders, and eating disorders. But more than anything, I bring my lived experience: I've been clean and sober for 13 years, and this work is deeply personal to me. I speak the language of recovery from the heart, and I'm passionate about reaching people in a way that makes them feel truly seen, heard, and supported.
At Hillside, we're more than just a treatment center — we're a family. Our home-like environment is warm, real, and full of life. We believe in laughing when we can, crying when we need to, and always showing up for our clients with compassion and honesty. We don't do cookie-cutter care — we do soul work, and we take it seriously.
Outside of work, I'm a proud mom, wife, sister, and daughter. I love animals, nature, music festivals, escape rooms, movies, and adventures that make great stories later. I live life out loud and love helping others do the same. Recovery gave me my life back — and I'm here to help others find theirs.

name: Nastasya Aracena
bio:
Nastasya (Nas) Aracena is a dedicated Clinical Therapist specializing in addiction and trauma, with extensive experience working across multiple levels of care, including PHP and IOP settings. She utilizes an integrative, compassionate, and evidence-based approach, incorporating Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), person-centered therapy, and solution-focused therapy to support clients in achieving sustainable recovery and emotional healing.
Nastasya is currently in the process of becoming EMDR-trained, further expanding her ability to facilitate deeper trauma resolution. She holds a CADC in the state of New Jersey and is actively advancing her clinical credentials while completing her Master's degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling with emphasis in trauma, finalizing her LCADC and LAC.
Her areas of interest include trauma recovery, addiction treatment, purpose-driven living, and the integration of mental health with personal and professional development. Nastasya's personal recovery journey—choosing to discontinue alcohol and other substances used during her adolescence—fuels her deep commitment to this work. This lived experience allows her to connect with clients authentically while modeling resilience, growth, and transformation. She is deeply passionate about empowering individuals to break through trauma and limiting patterns and build lives aligned with clarity, purpose, fulfillment and long-term success.

name: Nicholas Incognito Jr.
bio: Nicholas Incognito Jr. is a seasoned professional with over a decade of experience in the behavioral healthcare sector, currently serving as the Director of QA at Quadrant Health Group, Inc. In this role, he is at the forefront of providing a compassionate and streamlined entry point for individuals seeking treatment for substance abuse and mental health. His leadership philosophy centers on a blend of strategic oversight and hands-on guidance, ensuring that every client's journey begins with professionalism and empathy. Throughout his career, Nicholas has developed a strong reputation for optimizing operational efficiency and enhancing client outcomes. He has a proven track record of significantly improving conversion rates and client engagement, which has been instrumental in the growth and success of the admissions department. By fostering a collaborative environment, he ensures seamless coordination between his team, other internal departments, and external referral partners, creating a clear and supportive path to care for all clients. Passionate about reducing the barriers that prevent people from accessing necessary treatment, Nicholas is deeply committed to supporting individuals and their families through one of the most critical decisions of their lives. When he's not busy guiding his team or refining internal systems, he enjoys spending time outdoors and exploring creative outlets, which help him stay grounded and energized for the important work he does.

name: Norberto Segredo
bio:
Norberto serves as a dedicated Case Manager committed to empowering clients to achieve mastery over their mental health and recovery from substance use. He approaches his work with compassion, structure, and a deep belief in each individual's capacity for meaningful and lasting change.
His clinical perspective is grounded in his own recovery journey, which began in 2012. Having navigated both substance and process addictions alongside mental health challenges, Norberto brings a powerful lived-experience lens to his role. His personal transformation was further shaped through years of mentorship under the late Bob Proctor, where he studied the science of rewiring the mind to support sustainable growth and behavioral change.
Norberto specializes in integrating multiple recovery frameworks—including SMART Recovery, Dharma Recovery, and the 12-Step model—within a holistic wellness approach. He emphasizes a personalized 'toolkit' model, incorporating nutrition, meditation, exercise, and sauna therapy to strengthen resilience and long-term stability. With experience as both a peer specialist and group facilitator, his mission is to guide each client from simply surviving to truly thriving.

name: Olivia Hadjerioua
bio:
Olivia Hadjerioua serves as Executive Director for Dallas Detox Center and Fort Worth Wellness Center, where she oversees daily operations across both Texas facilities while supporting initiatives centered on quality care, team development, and client success.
Originally from Tennessee, Olivia brings a compassionate, people-centered approach to leadership rooted in her clinical background as a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC). With experience working across all levels of care, she is deeply passionate about helping individuals navigate mental health and substance use recovery with dignity, support, and hope.
In her leadership role, Olivia is committed to fostering a culture of accountability, integrity, and excellence while creating an environment where both clients and staff can thrive. Her dedication to operational excellence and individualized care continues to support the growth and mission of both Dallas Detox Center and Fort Worth Wellness Center.

name: Phillip Carter
bio:
Phillip Carter serves as the Director of Operations at Hillside Mission Recovery, bringing extensive experience in criminal justice, behavioral health, and recovery support. He began his career with the Indiana Department of Corrections, where he spent eight years working in state parole, case management, crisis intervention, and employment placement. During that time, he developed a strong foundation in meeting individuals where they are, navigating high-pressure situations with steadiness, and helping people recognize their potential—even when they struggle to see it themselves.
Phillip joined Hillside Mission Recovery as a Behavioral Health Technician and steadily advanced through leadership roles including Lead Tech and Case Manager before stepping into his current position as Director of Operations. He also spent two years managing sober living homes, an experience that strengthened his commitment to supporting individuals as they rebuild their lives with structure, accountability, and hope.
As Director of Operations, Phillip leads with integrity, grit, and compassion. He is deeply invested in cultivating a strong team culture and empowering staff to grow into their strengths. Seeing both clients and team members develop confidence, resilience, and purpose is what fuels his passion for the work. Phillip remains dedicated to helping create an environment where lasting recovery and meaningful transformation are possible.

name: Shawn Young
bio:
As the Executive Director of Southern California, I have the privilege of leading a team of dedicated professionals across several substance abuse treatment facilities — but at the heart of what I do is people. Whether it's helping a client take their first step toward recovery or supporting a staff member as they grow into leadership, my passion lies in developing others and building environments where people can thrive.
I didn't get here by accident. I worked my way up through this field — from cooking in the kitchen and working as a tech, to becoming a clinician, and now serving in executive leadership. That journey gave me a deep understanding of what this work really takes — grit, heart, and an unwavering commitment to showing up for people when they need it most.
I believe recovery is more than just treatment — it's a life long journey that we have the privilege of helping people build that foundation.
At the end of the day, I'm a husband and a father. My family is my foundation and the reason I lead with heart. The way I show up at home is how I try to show up in this work — grounded, honest, and fully present.
This isn't just a job to me — it's a calling, and I'm all in.

name: Shán Raiford
bio:
Shán Raiford is a National Certified Recovery Coach (NCRC) and experienced Case Manager with over a decade of service in the addiction treatment field. Since beginning his career in 2013, he has built a reputation for compassionate leadership, ethical practice, and results-driven client advocacy.
With a foundation in behavioral health, recovery coaching, and case management, Shán specializes in comprehensive needs assessment, individualized service planning, care coordination, and discharge planning across multiple levels of care. His systems-level understanding of treatment operations allows him to effectively navigate complex cases while ensuring each client receives structured, person-centered support.
Shán's approach is grounded in accountability, collaboration, and genuine human connection. He believes recovery is not just about sobriety—it is about rebuilding purpose, restoring dignity, and strengthening community.
His mission remains simple: empower individuals to transform adversity into growth and create sustainable, meaningful change.

name: Steven Ryan
bio:
Steven Ryan serves as the Director of Operations at Seaside Wellness Palm Beach, where he helps oversee the day-to-day operations of the facility and supports a treatment environment built on structure, accountability, and compassion. Guided by a strong belief in faith, responsibility, and meaningful connection, Steven brings both leadership and lived understanding to his role.
Steven's path into this work is deeply personal. At a pivotal point in his own life, someone stepped in to offer the guidance, structure, and support he needed to begin rebuilding. That experience continues to shape the way he leads today. He understands that recovery is not a one-time event, but a daily commitment to growth, purpose, and doing the next right thing.
In his role, Steven is focused on creating a safe, well-run, and supportive environment where clients can begin treatment with confidence. He is committed to helping both staff and clients stay grounded in the values that support lasting change, while ensuring the facility operates with consistency, care, and professionalism.

name: Tami Distefano
role: Program Director - Ocean Coast Recovery Center
bio:
I've been in this field for a little over 18 years, beginning my journey in 2007 by supporting Veterans. I started as a housing specialist and case manager, helping Veterans secure permanent housing and access their VA benefits. That experience opened the door for me to explore other meaningful ways to help people.
In 2013, I began working in treatment and discovered my passion for this work. I enrolled in school to become a drug and alcohol counselor and have since had the opportunity to work with a wide range of populations, including First Responders.
As someone in recovery myself, I bring a deep sense of understanding and empathy to the work I do. I know firsthand how challenging—and how rewarding—the journey can be. That experience allows me to meet people exactly where they are, with compassion, respect, and hope.
What I love most about working at Ocean Coast Recovery Center is the genuine care everyone here has—not just for their work, but for every person who comes through our doors. It truly feels like a family. I find purpose in connecting with each client and walking alongside them as they discover their own strength and work toward a life of sobriety.
Outside of work, I enjoy spending time with my family and taking beach days to reset and recharge. I'm passionate about sponsoring women in recovery and being part of their growth. I also love anything outdoors—traveling, ziplining, and skydiving whenever I get the chance. I believe in living fully, finding joy in the moment, and staying connected to what makes life meaningful.

name: Timothy Foley
bio:
Tim is a dedicated and compassionate Program Director with over seven years of experience in addiction treatment and behavioral health. He has developed and managed evidence-based, patient-centered programs and is skilled at leading multidisciplinary teams to deliver seamless, holistic care.
With a strong background in program evaluation and staff development, Tim ensures services are effective, up to date, and tailored to client needs. He holds a bachelor's degree in behavioral science and is known for his empathetic leadership style. Under his guidance, the program has achieved higher patient engagement and improved long-term recovery outcomes.

name: Vinny Turiello
bio: Vinny Turiello is the Client Care Coordinator Supervisor at Quadrant Health Group, where he has been a key part of the team since 2022. With over eight years of prior experience in public safety, Vinny brings a calm, solutions-focused approach to helping individuals and families navigate the challenges of seeking treatment for substance use and mental health concerns. At Quadrant, Vinny leads a dedicated team focused on client engagement, admissions support, and care coordination. His background in public safety gives him a unique perspective on crisis response, communication, and building trust with clients during vulnerable moments. Vinny is committed to ensuring that every client feels heard, respected, and supported from their first point of contact through their transition into care. His mission is to make accessing treatment not only possible—but personal.
